content:
- Read progress log, inspected .ralph/ralph.sh and .ralph/ralph-worker.sh for mode flow.
- Found ralph.sh already passes "$MODE" to ralph-worker.sh as argv1, but worker ignored argv and only used env MODE.
- Patched worker mode resolution to MODE="${1:-${MODE:-claude}}" so it now receives mode from ralph.sh while keeping env/default fallback.
- Next: if desired, run /bin/bash .ralph/ralph.sh codex (or claude/opencode) and confirm worker banner prints the expected mode.

- Investigated codex exit 127 in ralph worker; root cause is systemd-run PATH in .ralph/ralph.sh did not include ~/.npm-global/bin.
- Updated service PATH to prepend $HOME/.npm-global/bin so transient unit can resolve codex binary found by user at ~/.npm-global/bin/codex.
- Verified syntax with bash -n for both .ralph/ralph.sh and .ralph/ralph-worker.sh.
- Next: restart via /bin/bash .ralph/ralph.sh codex and confirm worker no longer logs "codex exited with code 127".

- Investigated OpenTelemetry export 404s from opentelemetry_sdk against http://10.0.4.30:5080/api/default.
- Located source in .ralph/ralph-worker.sh: OTEL endpoint was hardcoded to /api/default and codex was overriding exporter with a single otlp-http endpoint for all signals.
- Patched worker to define OTLP base endpoint and explicit signal endpoints:
  - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT=/api/default/v1/logs
  - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=/api/default/v1/traces
  - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT=/api/default/v1/metrics
  while keeping HTTP 5080 base endpoint.
- Removed codex otel.exporter override that forced single-path exports; now codex uses env OTEL config.
- For codex mode, set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS inline to stream-name=$CODEX_OTEL_NAME so each run streams to its own stream as before.
- Ran bash -n on .ralph/ralph-worker.sh (syntax ok).
- Could not network-probe 10.0.4.30 from sandbox (curl connect failed), so runtime verification must be done by rerunning worker and watching for absence of 404 errors.
- Next: run /bin/bash .ralph/ralph.sh codex and confirm no BatchLogProcessor/BatchSpanProcessor 404s to /api/default.

- Read previous progress log and parsed .ralph/current_tasks.md.
- Applied priority rules: there are many <passes>false</passes> tasks; story-rust-system-harness has ultra_high tasks, so STORY CHAIN RULE forces working this story in order.
- Selected first unblocked ultra_high task: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/01-task-core-types-time-errors-watch-channel.md and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt. Next cycle should execute this task.

- Read prior progress memory, read .ralph/current_task.txt, and opened task .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/01-task-core-types-time-errors-watch-channel.md.
- Per user gating workflow, performed parallel reconnaissance (plan doc + task inventory + repo layout), then wrote a full detailed execution plan into the task file.
- Added explicit phase gate marker `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file and stopped before any implementation work.
- Noted repo root currently has no top-level src/, so plan includes crate-root discovery before coding.
- Next turn should perform deep skeptical verification of the plan, alter at least one plan item, then switch gate to NOW EXECUTE when verified.

- User requested moving Codex OTEL setup out of .ralph/ralph-worker.sh and into .codex/config.toml using official Codex config docs.
- Read progress history, inspected current OTEL settings in ralph-worker.sh, then checked OpenAI Codex config docs for current keys: otel.exporter, otel.trace_exporter, protocol=json, headers map.
- Removed OTEL exporter/header/env juggling from ralph-worker.sh and removed codex -c OTEL overrides so codex relies on config file.
- Added .codex/config.toml with OpenObserve endpoints:
  - logs: http://10.0.4.30:5080/api/default/v1/logs
  - traces: http://10.0.4.30:5080/api/default/v1/traces
  with protocol="json", same Authorization header, and stream-name="pgtuskmaster_rust".
- Kept otel.log_user_prompt=true and set otel.environment="pgtuskmaster_rust" in config.
- Verified bash syntax for ralph-worker.sh with bash -n.
- Note: could not runtime-verify network export in sandbox; next step is run .ralph/ralph.sh codex and confirm telemetry arrives in OpenObserve stream pgtuskmaster_rust.
